content: Just as 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory systems prioritize selectively retaining only high-information, curiosity-prioritized experiences to avoid context bloat and improve long-term task performance, the emergent self-model of a general purpose AI agent can be reframed as an active learned memory schema that only updates self-representation when new information outperforms the predictive accuracy of existing self-beliefs. This turns the open-ended problem of agent self-identity into a tractable active learning problem grounded in modern memory SOTA.
